original_problem: There are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the lights on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb? Additionally, one of the bulbs is special and will only turn on if the switch is flipped an even number of times. If the special bulb is on when you enter the room, it will immediately turn off. You also know that the special bulb starts in the off position and its corresponding switch starts in the off position.

You can turn the lights on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb? Additionally, one of the bulbs is special and will only turn on ଯଦି (if) the switch is flipped an even number of times. ଯଦି (if) the special bulb is on when you enter the room, it will immediately turn off. You also know that the special bulb starts in the off position and its corresponding switch starts in the off position.
original_steps: Step 1: Turn switch 1 to the on position for 5 minutes to ensure that if it is the special bulb, it will be on when you enter the room.

Step 2: Turn switch 1 to the off position and turn switch 2 to the on position. This is to create a situation where you can identify the special bulb and its corresponding switch if it is either switch 1 or switch 2.

Step 3: Immediately enter the room to observe the bulbs. If one of the bulbs is on, it could be the special bulb if it was turned on an even number of times or a regular bulb. If the bulb that was on when you entered the room is now off, it must be the special bulb because it turns off immediately when observed if it was on.

Step 4: Identify the bulb that is on (if any) and the bulb that is off but warm (indicating it was recently on). If a bulb is on, it could be the special bulb or a regular bulb that was turned on. If a bulb is off but warm, it must have been the special bulb because it would have turned off when observed. The remaining bulb must be the one controlled by switch 3.

Step 5: Determine which switch controls which bulb based on the observations from step 4 and the initial setup. If the special bulb was identified in step 3, its switch can be identified. Then, by process of elimination, the remaining switches can be matched to the remaining bulbs.

The remaining bulb must be the one controlled by switch 3.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 5: Determine which switch controls which bulb based on the observations from ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 4 and the initial setup. ଯଦି (if) the special bulb was identified in ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3, its switch can be identified. ତାହେଲେ (then), by process of elimination, the remaining switches can be matched to the remaining bulbs.
